text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, Ye shall not go up neither shall ye fight against your brethren, return every man to his own house, for, from me, hath this thing been brought about. So they hearkened unto the words of Yahweh, and turned back from going against Jeroboam.
text.KJV: 11:4 Thus saith the Lord; Ye shall not goe vp, nor fight against your brethren: returne euery man to his house, for this thing is done of me. And they obeyed the words of the Lord, and returned from going against Ieroboam.
text.DRV: 11:4 Thus saith our Lord: You shall not go up, neither shall you fight against your brethren: let every man return into his house, because this thing is done by my will. Who when they had heard the word of our Lord, returned, neither went they forward against Jeroboam.

text.NRSV: Rehoboam resided in Jerusalem, and he built cities for defense in Judah.
text.Rotherham: And Rehoboam dwelt in Jerusalem,—and built cities for defence, in Judah;
text.KJV: 11:5 ¶ And Rehoboam dwelt in Ierusalem, and built cities for defence in Iudah.
text.DRV: 11:5 And Roboam dwelt in Jerusalem, and built walled cities in Juda.

text.NRSV: He built Bethlehem, Etam, Tekoa,
text.Rotherham: yea he built Bethlehem and Etam, and Tekoa;
text.KJV: 11:6 He built euen Bethlehem, and Etam, and Tekoa,
text.DRV: 11:6 And he built Bethlehem, and Etam, and Thecue,

text.NRSV: Beth-zur, Soco, Adullam,
text.Rotherham: and Beth-zur and Soco, and Adullam;
text.KJV: 11:7 And Bethzur, and Shoco, and Adullam,
text.DRV: 11:7 Bethsur also, and Socho, and Odollam,

text.NRSV: Gath, Mareshah, Ziph,
text.Rotherham: and Gath and Mareshah, and Ziph;
text.KJV: 11:8 And Gath, and Maresha, and Ziph,
text.DRV: 11:8 moreover also Geth, and Maresa, and Ziph,

text.NRSV: Adoraim, Lachish, Azekah,
text.Rotherham: and Adoraim and Lachish, and Azekah;
text.KJV: 11:9 And Adoraim, and Lachish, and Azekah,
text.DRV: 11:9 yea and Aduram, and Lachis, and Azeca,

text.NRSV: Zorah, Aijalon, and Hebron, fortified cities that are in Judah and in Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: and Zorah, and Aijalon and Hebron, which are in Judah and Benjamin,—as fortified cities.
text.KJV: 11:10 And Zorah, and Aialon, and Hebron, which are in Iudah and in Beniamin, fenced cities.
text.DRV: 11:10 Saraa also, and Aialon, and Hebron, which were in Juda and Benjamin, most fenced cities.

text.NRSV: He made the fortresses strong, and put commanders in them, and stores of food, oil, and wine.
text.Rotherham: And he strengthened the fortified places,—and put therein captains, and stores of food, and oil and wine;
text.KJV: 11:11 And he fortified the strong holds, and put captaines in them, and store of vitaile, and of oyle and wine.
text.DRV: 11:11 And when he had inclosed them with walls, he put in them Princes, and store houses of victuals, that is, of oil and wine.

text.NRSV: He found shields and spears in all the cities, and made them very strong. So he held Judah and Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: and, in every several city, shields and spears, and made them exceedingly strong,—thus Judah and Benjamin remained his.
text.KJV: 11:12 And in euery seuerall citie he put shields and speares, and made them exceeding strong, hauing Iudah and Beniamin on his side.
text.DRV: 11:12 Yea and in every city he made armories of shields and spears, and he strengthened them with great diligence, and reigned over Juda and Benjamin.

text.NRSV: The priests and the Levites who were in all Israel presented themselves to him from all their territories.
text.Rotherham: And, the priests and Levites that were in all Israel, took their stand with him, out of all their boundaries.
text.KJV: 11:13 ¶ And the Priests and the Leuites that were in all Israel, resorted to him out of all their coasts.
text.DRV: 11:13 And the Priests and Levites, that were in all Israel, came to him out of all their seats,

text.NRSV: The Levites had left their common lands and their holdings and had come to Judah and Jerusalem, because Jeroboam and his sons had prevented them from serving as priests of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: For the Levites left their pasture lands, and their possessions, and came to Judah, and to Jerusalem,—for Jeroboam and his sons cast them off, from ministering as priests unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 11:14 For the Leuites left their suburbs, and their possession, and came to Iudah and Ierusalem: for Ieroboam and his sonnes had cast them off from executing the Priests office vnto the Lord.
text.DRV: 11:14 leaving their suburbs, and their possessions, and passing to Juda, and Jerusalem, because Jeroboam had cast them off, and their posterity: that they should not execute the priesthood of our Lord.

text.NRSV: and had appointed his own priests for the high places, and for the goat-idols, and for the calves that he had made.
text.Rotherham: and appointed for himself priests for the high places, and for the demons—and for the calves which he had made.
text.KJV: 11:15 And hee ordeined him priests for the high places, and for the deuils, and for the calues which he had made.
text.DRV: 11:15 Who made unto himself Priests of the excelses, and of devils, and of the calves which he had made.

text.NRSV: Those from all the tribes of Israel who had set their hearts to seek the Lord God of Israel came after them to Jerusalem to sacrifice to the Lord, the God of their ancestors.
text.Rotherham: And, after them—out of all the tribes of Israel, such as were setting their heart to seek Yahweh God of Israel, came to Jerusalem, to sacrifice unto Yahweh, God of their fathers.
text.KJV: 11:16 And after them out of all the tribes of Israel, such as set their hearts to seeke the Lord God of Israel, came to Ierusalem, to sacrifice vnto the Lord God of their fathers.
text.DRV: 11:16 Yea and of all the tribes of Israel, whosoever had given their heart to seek our Lord the God of Israel, came into Jerusalem to immolate their victims before our Lord the God of their fathers.

text.NRSV: They strengthened the kingdom of Judah, and for three years they made Rehoboam son of Solomon strong, for they walked for three years in the way of David and Solomon.
text.Rotherham: So they strengthened the kingdom of Judah, and emboldened Rehoboam son of Solomon, for three years,—for they walked in the way of David and Solomon, for three years.
text.KJV: 11:17 So they strengthened the kingdome of Iudah, and made Rehoboam the sonne of Solomon strong, three yeeres: for three yeeres they walked in the way of Dauid and Solomon.
text.DRV: 11:17 And they strengthened the Kingdom of Juda, and established Roboam the son of Salomon for three years: for they walked in the ways of David and Salomon, only three years.

text.NRSV: Rehoboam took as his wife Mahalath daughter of Jerimoth son of David, and of Abihail daughter of Eliab son of Jesse.
text.Rotherham: And Rehoboam took him to wife, Mahalath, daughter of Jerimoth, son of David,—and Abihail, daughter of Eliab, son of Jesse;
text.KJV: 11:18 ¶ And Rehoboam tooke him Mahalath the daughter of Ierimoth the sonne of Dauid to wife, and Abihail the daughter of Eliab the son of Iesse:
text.DRV: 11:18 And Roboam took to wife Mahalath, the daughter of Jerimoth the son of David: Abihail also the daughter of Eliab the son of Isai,

text.NRSV: She bore him sons: Jeush, Shemariah, and Zaham.
text.Rotherham: and she bare him sons,—Jeush and Shamariah and Zaham.
text.KJV: 11:19 Which bare him children, Ieush, and Shamariah, and Zaham.
text.DRV: 11:19 who bare him sons, Jehus, & Somorias, and Zoom.

text.NRSV: After her he took Maacah daughter of Absalom, who bore him Abijah, Attai, Ziza, and Shelomith.
text.Rotherham: And, after her, he took Maacah, daughter of Absolom,—and she bare him Abijah, and Attai, and Ziza, and Shelomith.
text.KJV: 11:20 And after her, hee tooke Maacah the daughter of Absalom, which bare him Abiiah, and Atthai, and Ziza, and Shelomith.
text.DRV: 11:20 After this woman also he took Maacha the daughter of Absalom, who bare him Abia, and Ethai, and Ziza, & Salomith.

text.NRSV: Rehoboam loved Maacah daughter of Absalom more than all his other wives and concubines (he took eighteen wives and sixty concubines, and became the father of twenty-eight sons and sixty daughters).
text.Rotherham: And Rehoboam loved Maacah daughter of Absolom, above all his wives and his concubines, for, eighteen wives, took he, and sixty concubines,—and begat twenty-eight sons, and sixty daughters.
text.KJV: 11:21 And Rehoboam loued Maacah the daughter of Absalom, aboue all his wiues and his concubines: for he tooke eighteene wiues, and threescore concubines, and begate twentie and eight sonnes, and threescore daughters.
text.DRV: 11:21 And Roboam loved Maacha the daughter of Absalom above all his wives and concubines: for he had married eighteen wives, and threescore concubines: and he begat eight and twenty sons, and threescore daughters.

text.NRSV: Rehoboam appointed Abijah son of Maacah as chief prince among his brothers, for he intended to make him king.
text.Rotherham: So then Rehoboam appointed Abijah son of Maacah as head, to be chief ruler among his brethren,—yea that he might make him king.
text.KJV: 11:22 And Rehoboam made Abiiah the sonne of Maacah the chiefe, to be ruler among his brethren: for he thought to make him king.
text.DRV: 11:22 But he appointed for head Abias the son of Maacha duke over all his brethren: for he meant to make him king,

text.NRSV: He dealt wisely, and distributed some of his sons through all the regions of Judah and Benjamin, in all the fortified cities; he gave them abundant provisions, and found many wives for them.
text.Rotherham: So he took heed, and dispersed all his sons throughout all the lands of Judah and Benjamin, unto all the fortified cities, and gave them food in abundance,—and asked a multitude of wives.
text.KJV: 11:23 And he dealt wisely, and dispersed of all his children throughout all the countries of Iudah and Beniamin, vnto euery fenced citie: and he gaue them vitaile in abundance: and hee desired many wiues.
text.DRV: 11:23 because he was wiser, and mightier above all his sons, and in all the coasts of Juda, and of Benjamin, and in all the walled cities: and he gave them much meat, and he desired many wives.

text.NRSV: When the rule of Rehoboam was established and he grew strong, he abandoned the law of the Lord, and all Israel with him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Rehoboam had established the kingdom, and had strengthened himself, he forsook the law of Yahweh,—and all Israel with him.
text.KJV: 12:1 And it came to passe when Rehoboam had established the kingdome, and had strengthened himselfe, hee forsooke the Law of the Lord, and all Israel with him.
text.DRV: 12:1 And when the kingdom of Roboam was strengthened and fortified, he forsook the law of our Lord, and all Israel with him.

text.NRSV: In the fifth year of King Rehoboam, because they had been unfaithful to the Lord, King Shishak of Egypt came up against Jerusalem
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, in the fifth year of King Rehoboam, that Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem,—because they had dealt treacherously against Yahweh;—
text.KJV: 12:2 And it came to passe, that in the fifth yere of Rehoboam, Shishak king of Egypt came vp against Ierusalem, ( because they had transgressed against the Lord )
text.DRV: 12:2 And in the fifth year of the kingdom of Roboam came up Sesac the king of Ægypt into Jerusalem (because they had sinned to our Lord)

text.NRSV: with twelve hundred chariots and sixty thousand cavalry. A countless army came with him from Egypt—Libyans, Sukkiim, and Ethiopians.
text.Rotherham: with twelve hundred chariots, and with sixty thousand horsemen,-and, without number, the people who came with him out of Egypt—Lybians, Sukkiim, and Ethiopians.
text.KJV: 12:3 With twelue hundred charets, and threescore thousand horsemen: and the people were without number that came with him out of Egypt: the Lubims, the Sukkiims, & the Ethiopians.
text.DRV: 12:3 with a thousand two hundred chariots, and threescore thousand horsemen: neither was any number of the common people, that came with him out of Ægypt, to wit, Libyans, and Troglodytes, and Æthiopians.

text.NRSV: He took the fortified cities of Judah and came as far as Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And he captured the cities of defence which belonged to Judah,—and came, as far as Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 12:4 And hee tooke the fenced cities which perteined to Iudah, and came to Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 12:4 And he took the most fenced cities in Juda, and came over unto Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Shemaiah came to Rehoboam and to the officers of Judah, who had gathered at Jerusalem because of Shishak, and said to them, “Thus says the Lord: You abandoned me, so I have abandoned you to the hand of Shishak.”
text.Rotherham: And, Shemaiah the prophet, came unto Rehoboam, and the rulers of Judah, who had gathered themselves together unto Jerusalem because of Shishak,—and said unto them, Thus, saith Yahweh, Ye, have left, me, Therefore, I also, have left, you, in the hands of Shishak.
text.KJV: 12:5 ¶ Then came Shemaiah the prophet to Rehoboam, and to the Princes of Iudah that were gathered together to Ierusalem because of Shishak, and said vnto them, Thus saith the Lord, Ye haue forsaken me, and therfore haue I also left you in the hand of Shishak.
text.DRV: 12:5 And Semeias the Prophet went to Roboam, and to the Princes of Juda, that were gathered together in Jerusalem, flying from Sesac, and he said to them: Thus saith our Lord: You have left me, and I have left you in the hand of Sesac.

text.NRSV: Then the officers of Israel and the king humbled themselves and said, “The Lord is in the right.”
text.Rotherham: Then the rulers of Israel and the king humbled themselves,—and said, Righteous, is Yahweh!
text.KJV: 12:6 Whereupon, the Princes of Israel, and the king humbled themselues and they saide, The Lord is righteous.
text.DRV: 12:6 And the Princes of Israel, and the king, being astonished, said: Our Lord is just.

text.NRSV: When the Lord saw that they humbled themselves, the word of the Lord came to Shemaiah, saying: “They have humbled themselves; I will not destroy them, but I will grant them some deliverance, and my wrath shall not be poured out on Jerusalem by the hand of Shishak.
text.Rotherham: And, when Yahweh saw that they humbled themselves, the word of Yahweh came unto Shemaiah, saying—They have humbled themselves, I will not destroy them,—but will grant them, in a little while, to escape, and my wrath shall not be poured out upon Jerusalem, by the hand of Shishak.
text.KJV: 12:7 And when the Lord saw that they humbled themselues, the word of the Lord came to Shemaiah, saying, They haue humbled themselues, therefore I will not destroy them, but I will grant them some deliuerance, and my wrath shall not bee powred out vpon Ierusalem, by the hand of Shishak.
text.DRV: 12:7 And when our Lord had seen that they were humbled, the word of our Lord came to Semeias, saying: Because they are humbled, I will not destroy them, and I will give them a little aid, & my fury shall not drop upon Jerusalem by the hand of Sesac.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless they shall be his servants, so that they may know the difference between serving me and serving the kingdoms of other lands.”
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless they shall become his servants, that they may know my service, and the service of the kingdoms of the countries.
text.KJV: 12:8 Neuerthelesse they shalbe his seruants, that they may know my seruice, and the seruice of the kingdomes of the countreys.
text.DRV: 12:8 But yet they shall serve him, that they may know the distance of my service, and of the service of the Kingdom of the earth.

text.NRSV: So King Shishak of Egypt came up against Jerusalem; he took away the treasures of the house of the Lord and the treasures of the king’s house; he took everything. He also took away the shields of gold that Solomon had made;
text.Rotherham: So Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem, and took the treasures of the house of Yahweh, and the treasures of the house of the king, the whole, he took,—and he took the bucklers of gold, which Solomon had made.
text.KJV: 12:9 So Shishak king of Egypt came vp against Ierusalem, and tooke away the treasures of the house of the Lord, and the treasures of the kings house, hee tooke all: he caried away also the shields of gold, which Solomon had made.
text.DRV: 12:9 Therefore Sesac the king of Ægypt retired from Jerusalem, taking away the treasures of the house of our Lord, and of the king's house, & he took all things with him, and the golden shields that Salomon had made,

text.NRSV: but King Rehoboam made in their place shields of bronze, and committed them to the hands of the officers of the guard, who kept the door of the king’s house.
text.Rotherham: And King Rehoboam made, instead of them, bucklers of bronze,—and committed them unto the hand of the captains of the runners, who kept guard at the entrance of the house of the king.
text.KJV: 12:10 In stead of which, king Rehoboam made shields of brasse, and committed them to the hands of the chiefe of the guard, that kept the entrance of the Kings house.
text.DRV: 12:10 for the which the king made brazen ones, and delivered them to the Princes of the shield-bearers, which kept the entrance of the palace.

text.NRSV: Whenever the king went into the house of the Lord, the guard would come and carry them, and would then bring them back to the guardroom.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, whensoever the king came into the house of Yahweh, the runners came and bare them, and then returned them into the chamber of the runners.
text.KJV: 12:11 And when the king entred into the house of the Lord, the guard came and fet them, and brought them againe into the guard-chamber.
text.DRV: 12:11 And when the king entered into the house of our Lord, the shield-bearers came, and took them, and brought them back again to their armoury.

text.NRSV: When he humbled himself, the wrath of the Lord turned from him, so as not to destroy them completely; moreover, conditions were good in Judah.
text.Rotherham: But, when he humbled himself, then turned from him the anger of Yahweh, that he would not destroy, to make an end,—moreover also, in Judah, there were some good things.
text.KJV: 12:12 And when he humbled himselfe, the wrath of the Lord turned from him, that hee would not destroy him altogether: and also in Iudah things went well.
text.DRV: 12:12 But yet because they were humbled, the wrath of our Lord was turned away from them, neither were they utterly destroyed: for in Juda there were found good works.

text.NRSV: So King Rehoboam established himself in Jerusalem and reigned. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign; he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city that the Lord had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel to put his name there. His mother’s name was Naamah the Ammonite.
text.Rotherham: So King Rehoboam strengthened himself in Jerusalem, and reigned,—because, forty-one years old, was Rehoboam when he began to reign, and, seventeen years, reigned he in Jerusalem, the city which Yahweh had chosen, to set his Name there—from among all the tribes of Israel, and, the name of his mother, was Naamah, the Ammonitess.
text.KJV: 12:13 ¶ So king Rehoboam strengthened himselfe in Ierusalem, and reigned: for Rehoboam was one and fourty yeeres olde when hee began to reigne, and he reigned seuenteen yeeres in Ierusalem, the citie which the Lord had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, to put his Name there: and his mothers name was Naamah an Ammonitesse.
text.DRV: 12:13 King Roboam therefore was strengthened in Jerusalem, and reigned: one & forty years old was he when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city which our Lord chose, to confirm his name there, out of all the tribes of Israel: and the name of his mother was Naama an Ammonite.

text.NRSV: He did evil, for he did not set his heart to seek the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But he did evil,—in that he did not fix his heart to seek Yahweh.
text.KJV: 12:14 And hee did euill, because hee prepared not his heart to seeke the Lord.
text.DRV: 12:14 And he did evil, and prepared not his heart to seek our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now the acts of Rehoboam, from first to last, are they not written in the records of the prophet Shemaiah and of the seer Iddo, recorded by genealogy? There were continual wars between Rehoboam and Jeroboam.
text.Rotherham: Now, the story of Rehoboam, first and last, is it not written in the story of Shemaiah the prophet and Iddo the seer, for enrolling,-also the wars of Rehoboam and Jeroboam, all the days?
text.KJV: 12:15 Now the acts of Rehoboam first and last, are they not written in the booke of Shemaiah the Prophet, and of Iddo the Seer, concerning genealogies? and there were warres betweene Rehoboam & Ieroboam continually.
text.DRV: 12:15 But the works of Roboam the first and the last are written in the Books of Semeias the Prophet, and of Addo the Seer, and diligently expounded: and Roboam and Jeroboam fought one against the other all their days.

text.NRSV: Rehoboam slept with his ancestors and was buried in the city of David; and his son Abijah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Rehoboam slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David,—and Abijah his son reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: 12:16 And Rehoboam slept with his fathers, and was buried in the citie of Dauid, and Abiiah his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.DRV: 12:16 And Roboam slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David. And Abias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam, Abijah began to reign over Judah.
text.Rotherham: In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam, began Abijah to reign over Judah:
text.KJV: 13:1 Nowe in the eighteenth yeere of king Ieroboam, began Abiiah to reigne ouer Iudah.
text.DRV: 13:1 In the eighteenth year of king Jeroboam, reigned Abias over Juda.

text.NRSV: He reigned for three years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Micaiah daughter of Uriel of Gibeah. Now there was war between Abijah and Jeroboam.
text.Rotherham: three years, reigned he in Jerusalem, and, the name of his mother, was Maacah, daughter of Uriel of Gibeah,—and there was, war, between Abijah and Jeroboam.
text.KJV: 13:2 He reigned three yeres in Ierusalem: (his mothers name also was Michaiah the daughter of Uriel of Gibea:) and there was warre between Abiiah and Ieroboam.
text.DRV: 13:2 Three years reigned he in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Michaia, the daughter of Uriel of Gabaa: there was war between Abias & Jeroboam.

text.NRSV: Abijah went out to battle having an army of valiant warriors, four hundred thousand picked men; and Jeroboam drew up his line of battle against him with eight hundred thousand picked mighty warriors.
text.Rotherham: And Abijah began the war with a force of heroes of war, four hundred thousand chosen men,—and, Jeroboam, set in array against him to battle, with eight hundred thousand chosen men, heroes of valour.
text.KJV: 13:3 And Abiiah set the battel in aray with an army of valiant men of warre, euen foure hundred thousand chosen men: Ieroboam also set the battell in aray against him with eight hundred thousand chosen men, being mightie men of valour.
text.DRV: 13:3 And when Abias had begun battle, and had most warlike men, and of chosen ones four hundreth thousand: Jeroboam put his army in array on the contrary side, eight hundreth thousand men, who themselves also were chosen men, & most valiant to battles.

text.NRSV: Then Abijah stood on the slope of Mount Zemaraim that is in the hill country of Ephraim, and said, “Listen to me, Jeroboam and all Israel!
text.Rotherham: And Abijah stood up upon Mount Zemaraim, which is in the hill country of Ephraim,—and said, Hear me, O Jeroboam and all Israel!
text.KJV: 13:4 ¶ And Abiiah stood vp vpon mount Zemaraim, which is in mount Ephraim, and sayde, Heare mee thou Ieroboam, and all Israel:
text.DRV: 13:4 Abias therefore stood upon mount Semeron, which was in Ephraim, and said: Hear Jeroboam, & all Israel:

text.NRSV: Do you not know that the Lord God of Israel gave the kingship over Israel forever to David and his sons by a covenant of salt?
text.Rotherham: Is it not yours to know, that, Yahweh God of Israel gave the kingdom to David, over Israel, unto times age-abiding,—to him and to his sons, by a covenant of salt?
text.KJV: 13:5 Ought you not to know, that the Lord God of Israel gaue the kingdome ouer Israel to Dauid for euer, euen to him and to his sonnes by a couenant of salt?
text.DRV: 13:5 Are you ignorant that our Lord the God of Israel gave the kingdom to David over Israel for ever, to him & his children as a covenant of salt?

text.NRSV: Yet Jeroboam son of Nebat, a servant of Solomon son of David, rose up and rebelled against his lord;
text.Rotherham: Yet hath Jeroboam son of Nebat, servant of Solomon son of David, risen up,—and rebelled against his lord.
text.KJV: 13:6 Yet Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, the seruant of Solomon the sonne of Dauid, is risen vp, and hath rebelled against his Lord.
text.DRV: 13:6 And there rose up Jeroboam the son of Nabat, the servant of Salomon the son of David: and rebelled against his Lord.

text.NRSV: and certain worthless scoundrels gathered around him and defied Rehoboam son of Solomon, when Rehoboam was young and irresolute and could not withstand them.
text.Rotherham: And there are gathered unto him vain men, sons of the Abandoned One, who emboldened themselves against Rehoboam son of Solomon,-when, Rehoboam, was young and tender of heart, and had not strengthened himself to meet them.
text.KJV: 13:7 And there are gathered vnto him vaine men the children of Belial, and haue strengthened themselues against Rehoboam the sonne of Solomon, when Rehoboam was young, & tender hearted, and could not withstand them.
text.DRV: 13:7 And there were gathered to him all the most vain men, and the children of Belial: and they prevailed against Roboam the son of Salomon: moreover Roboam was rude, and of a fearful heart, and could not resist them.

text.NRSV: “And now you think that you can withstand the kingdom of the Lord in the hand of the sons of David, because you are a great multitude and have with you the golden calves that Jeroboam made as gods for you.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye, are thinking to strengthen yourselves against the kingdom of Yahweh, in the hand of the sons of David,—and, ye, are a great multitude, and, with you, are calves of gold, which Jeroboam hath made you for gods.
text.KJV: 13:8 And now ye thinke to withstand the kingdome of the Lord, in the hand of the sonnes of Dauid, and ye be a great multitude, and there are with you golden calues, which Ieroboam made you for gods.
text.DRV: 13:8 Now therefore you say that you are able to resist the Kingdom of our Lord, which he possesseth by the children of David, and you have a great multitude of people, and golden calves, which Jeroboam hath made you for gods.

text.NRSV: Have you not driven out the priests of the Lord, the descendants of Aaron, and the Levites, and made priests for yourselves like the peoples of other lands? Whoever comes to be consecrated with a young bull or seven rams becomes a priest of what are no gods.
text.Rotherham: Have ye not driven out the priests of Yahweh, the sons of Aaron, and the Levites,—and made for yourselves priests like the peoples of the countries? Whosoever cometh to install himself with a young bullock, and seven rams, then becometh he a priest unto the, no-gods.
text.KJV: 13:9 Haue yee not cast out the Priests of the Lord the sonnes of Aaron, and the Leuites, and haue made you priests after the maner of the nations of other lands? so that whosoeuer commeth to consecrate himselfe with a young bullocke and seuen rammes, the same may be a priest of them that are no gods.
text.DRV: 13:9 And you have cast out the Priests of our Lord, the children of Aaron, & the Levites: and you have made you Priests, as all the peoples of the earth: who soever shall come and consecrate his hand in a bullock of oxen, and in seven rams, is made the Priest of them that are not gods.

text.NRSV: But as for us, the Lord is our God, and we have not abandoned him. We have priests ministering to the Lord who are descendants of Aaron, and Levites for their service.
text.Rotherham: But, as for us, Yahweh, is our God, and we have not forsaken him,—and, the priests who are waiting upon Yahweh, are sons of Aaron, with Levites in the work;
text.KJV: 13:10 But as for vs, the Lord is our God, and wee haue not forsaken him, and the Priests which minister vnto the Lord, are the sonnes of Aaron, and the Leuites waite vpon their businesse.
text.DRV: 13:10 But our Lord is God, whom we forsake not, and the Priests do minister to our Lord of the children of Aaron, and the Levites are in their order.

text.NRSV: They offer to the Lord every morning and every evening burnt offerings and fragrant incense, and set forth the rows of bread on the table of pure gold, and care for the golden lampstand so that its lamps may burn every evening; for we keep the charge of the Lord our God, but you have abandoned him.
text.Rotherham: and they are making a perfume unto Yahweh, with ascending-sacrifices morning by morning, and evening by evening and an incense of sweet spices, and are putting in order bread upon the pure table, and the lampstand of gold with the lamps thereof, for lighting up evening by evening, for, observant, are we of the charge of Yahweh our God,—whereas, ye, have forsaken him.
text.KJV: 13:11 And they burne vnto the Lord euery morning, and euery euening, burnt sacrifices and sweete incense: the shew-bread also set they in order vpon the pure table, and the Candlesticke of golde with the lampes therof, to burne euery euening: for we keepe the charge of the Lord our God, but yee haue forsaken him.
text.DRV: 13:11 Holocausts also they do offer to our Lord, every day morning and evening, and incense made according to the precepts of the law, and the loaves are set forth on a most clean table, and there is with us the golden candlestick, and the lamps thereof, that they may be lighted always at evening: for we keep the precepts of the Lord our God, whom you have forsaken.

text.NRSV: See, God is with us at our head, and his priests with their battle trumpets to sound the call to battle against you. O Israelites, do not fight against the Lord, the God of your ancestors; for you cannot succeed.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! with us as Head, is God himself, and his priests, and the trumpets of alarm, to sound an alarm against you,—O sons of Israel! do not fight against Yahweh God of your fathers, for ye shall not prosper.
text.KJV: 13:12 And behold, God himselfe is with vs for our captaine, and his Priests with sounding trumpets to cry alarme against you: O children of Israel, fight ye not against the Lord God of your fathers, for you shall not prosper.
text.DRV: 13:12 Therefore in our host God is the Prince, & his Priests, which sound with trumpets, and resound against you: children of Israel fight not against our Lord the God of your fathers, because it is not expedient for you.

text.NRSV: Jeroboam had sent an ambush around to come on them from behind; thus his troops were in front of Judah, and the ambush was behind them.
text.Rotherham: But, Jeroboam, sent round an ambush, to come up from behind them,—so they were before Judah and, the ambush, did come up from behind them.
text.KJV: 13:13 ¶ But Ieroboam caused an ambushment to come about behinde them: so they were before Iudah, and the ambushment was behind them.
text.DRV: 13:13 He speaking these things, Jeroboam endeavoured to entrap him behind. And when he stood over against the enemies, he compassed Juda unwitting with his army.

text.NRSV: When Judah turned, the battle was in front of them and behind them. They cried out to the Lord, and the priests blew the trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And, when Judah turned and lo! as for them, the battle was before and behind, then made they outcry unto Yahweh,—and, the priests, kept on blowing with the trumpets.
text.KJV: 13:14 And when Iudah looked backe, behold, the battel was before and behind; and they cried vnto the Lord, and the Priests sounded with the trumpets.
text.DRV: 13:14 And Judas looking back, saw the battle at hand before and behind, and cried to our Lord: and the Priests began to sound with trumpets.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah raised the battle shout. And when the people of Judah shouted, God defeated Jeroboam and all Israel before Abijah and Judah.
text.Rotherham: Then the men of Judah gave a shout,—and it came to pass, when the men of Judah shouted, then, God himself, smote Jeroboam and all Israel, before Abijah and Judah.
text.KJV: 13:15 Then the men of Iudah gaue a shout: and as the men of Iudah shouted, it came to passe that God smote Ieroboam and all Israel, before Abiiah and Iudah.
text.DRV: 13:15 And all the men of Juda made a shout: and behold they crying, God terrified Jeroboam, and all Israel that stood against Abias and Juda.

text.NRSV: The Israelites fled before Judah, and God delivered them into their hands.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel fled from before Judah,—and God delivered them into their hand.
text.KJV: 13:16 And the children of Israel fled before Iudah: and God deliuered them into their hand.
text.DRV: 13:16 And the children of Israel fled from Juda, and our Lord delivered them into their hand.

text.NRSV: Abijah and his army defeated them with great slaughter; five hundred thousand picked men of Israel fell slain.
text.Rotherham: And Abijah and his people smote among them with a great smiting,—and there fell down slain, of Israel, five hundred thousand chosen men.
text.KJV: 13:17 And Abiiah and his people slew them with a great slaughter: so there fel downe slaine of Israel, fiue hundred thousand chosen men.
text.DRV: 13:17 Abias therefore and his people struck them with a great slaughter, and there fell wounded of Israel five hundreth thousand valiant men.

text.NRSV: Thus the Israelites were subdued at that time, and the people of Judah prevailed, because they relied on the Lord, the God of their ancestors.
text.Rotherham: Thus were the sons of Israel subdued at that time,—and the sons of Judah prevailed, because they leaned upon Yahweh the God of their fathers.
text.KJV: 13:18 Thus the children of Israel were brought vnder at that time, and the children of Iudah preuailed, because they relied vpon the Lord God of their fathers.
text.DRV: 13:18 And the children of Israel were humbled at that time, and the children of Juda exceedingly encouraged, because they had trusted in our Lord the God of their fathers.

text.NRSV: Abijah pursued Jeroboam, and took cities from him: Bethel with its villages, Jeshanah with its villages, and Ephron with its villages.
text.Rotherham: And Abijah pursued after Jeroboam, and captured from him, cities, even Bethel, with the villages thereof, and Jeshanah, with the villages thereof,—and Ephron, with the villages thereof;
text.KJV: 13:19 And Abiiah pursued after Ieroboam, & tooke cities from him, Beth-el with the townes thereof, and Ieshanah with the townes thereof, and Ephrain with the townes thereof.
text.DRV: 13:19 And Abias pursued Jeroboam flying, and he took his cities, Bethel and her daughters, & Jesana with her daughters, Ephron also and her daughters.

text.NRSV: Jeroboam did not recover his power in the days of Abijah; the Lord struck him down, and he died.
text.Rotherham: neither was Jeroboam strong any more, in the days of Abijah,—and Yahweh smote him that he died.
text.KJV: 13:20 Neither did Ieroboam recouer strength againe in the dayes of Abiiah: and the Lord strooke him, & he died.
text.DRV: 13:20 Neither was Jeroboam able to resist any more, in the days of Abias: whom our Lord struck, and he died.

text.NRSV: But Abijah grew strong. He took fourteen wives, and became the father of twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.
text.Rotherham: And Abijah strengthened himself, and took him, fourteen wives,—and begat twenty-two sons, and sixteen daughters.
text.KJV: 13:21 ¶ But Abiiah waxed mighty, and married fourteene wiues, and begate twentie and two sonnes, and sixteene daughters.
text.DRV: 13:21 Therefore Abias, his empire being strengthened, took fourteen wives: and he begat two and twenty sons, & sixteen daughters.

text.NRSV: The rest of the acts of Abijah, his behavior and his deeds, are written in the story of the prophet Iddo.
text.Rotherham: And, the rest of the story of Abijah, both his ways and his words,—are written, in the commentary of the prophet Iddo.
text.KJV: 13:22 And the rest of the acts of Abiiah, and his waies, and his sayings, are written in the story of the Prophet Iddo.
text.DRV: 13:22 But the rest of the words of Abias, and of his ways and words, are written diligently in the Book of Addo the Prophet.

text.NRSV: Abijah slept with his ancestors, and they buried him in the city of David. His son Asa succeeded him. In his days the land had rest for ten years.
text.Rotherham: So Abijah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city of David, and Asa his son reigned in his stead,—in his days, the land rested ten years.
text.KJV: 14:1 So Abiiah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the citie of Dauid, and Asa his sonne reigned in his stead: in his dayes the land was quiet ten yeeres.
text.DRV: 14:1 And Abias slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the City of David: & Asa his son reigned for him, in whose days the land was quiet ten years.

text.NRSV: Asa did what was good and right in the sight of the Lord his God.
text.Rotherham: And Asa did that which was good and that which was right, in the eyes of Yahweh his God;
text.KJV: 14:2 And Asa did that which was good and right in the eyes of the Lord his God.
text.DRV: 14:2 And Asa did that which was good and pleasing in the sight of his God, & he overthrew the altars of strange service, and the excelses,

text.NRSV: He took away the foreign altars and the high places, broke down the pillars, hewed down the sacred poles,
text.Rotherham: and took away the foreign altars, and the high places,—and brake in pieces the pillars, and cut in twain the Sacred Stems;
text.KJV: 14:3 For hee tooke away the altars of the strange gods, and the high places, and brake downe the images, and cut downe the groues:
text.DRV: 14:3 and brake the statues, and cut down the groves.

text.NRSV: and commanded Judah to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and to keep the law and the commandment.
text.Rotherham: and bade Judah seek Yahweh, God of their fathers,-and execute the law, and the commandment;
text.KJV: 14:4 And commanded Iudah to seeke the Lord God of their fathers, and to do the Law, and the Commandement.
text.DRV: 14:4 And he commanded Juda that they should seek our Lord the God of their fathers, and should do the law, and all the commandments.

text.NRSV: He also removed from all the cities of Judah the high places and the incense altars. And the kingdom had rest under him.
text.Rotherham: and removed from all the cities of Judah, the high places, and the sun-images,—and the kingdom became quiet before him.
text.KJV: 14:5 Also he tooke away out of all the cities of Iudah, the high places and the images: and the kingdome was quiet before him.
text.DRV: 14:5 And he took away out of all the cities of Juda the altars, & temples, and reigned in peace.

text.NRSV: He built fortified cities in Judah while the land had rest. He had no war in those years, for the Lord gave him rest.
text.Rotherham: And he built cities of defence, in Judah,—for the land had quiet, nor was there near him any war, in these years, because Yahweh had given him rest.
text.KJV: 14:6 ¶ And hee built fenced cities in Iudah; for the land had rest, and hee had no warre in those yeeres; because the Lord had giuen him rest.
text.DRV: 14:6 He built also fenced cities in Juda, because he was quiet, and there had no battles risen in his time, our Lord giving peace.

text.NRSV: He said to Judah, “Let us build these cities, and surround them with walls and towers, gates and bars; the land is still ours because we have sought the Lord our God; we have sought him, and he has given us rest on every side.” So they built and prospered.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said he to Judah, Let us build these cities, and let us surround them with walls and towers, doors and bars, while the land is yet before us, for we have sought Yahweh our God, we have sought him, and he hath given us rest on every side. So they built, and prospered.
text.KJV: 14:7 Therefore hee said vnto Iudah, Let vs build these cities, & make about them walles, and towers, gates and barres, while the land is yet before vs: because wee haue sought the Lord our God, wee haue sought him, and hee hath giuen vs rest on euery side: so they built, and prospered.
text.DRV: 14:7 And he said to Juda: let us build these cities, and compass them with walls, and strengthen them with towers, and gates, & locks, whilst things are quiet from battles, because we have sought our Lord the God of our fathers, and he hath given us peace round about. They therefore did build, and there was no impediment in building.

text.NRSV: Asa had an army of three hundred thousand from Judah, armed with large shields and spears, and two hundred eighty thousand from Benjamin that carried shields and drew bows; all these were mighty warriors.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Asa, had a force bearing shield and spear, out of Judah, three hundred thousand, and, out of Benjamin, such as bare a buckler and trode a bow, two hundred and eighty thousand,—all these, were heroes of valour.
text.KJV: 14:8 And Asa had an armie of men that bare targets and speares, out of Iudah three hundred thousand, and out of Beniamin, that bare shields and drew bowes, two hundred and fourescore thousand: all these were mighty men of valour.
text.DRV: 14:8 And Asa had in his army of them that carried shields and spears, of Juda three hundred thousand: and of Benjamin shield-bearers and archers, two hundred eighty thousand, all these were most valiant men.

text.NRSV: Zerah the Ethiopian came out against them with an army of a million men and three hundred chariots, and came as far as Mareshah.
text.Rotherham: Then came out against them Zerah the Ethiopian, with a force of a thousand thousand, and three hundred chariots,—and he came as far as Mareshah.
text.KJV: 14:9 ¶ And there came out against them Zerah the Ethiopian, with an host of a thousand thousand, and three hundred charets, and came vnto Mareshah.
text.DRV: 14:9 And Zara the Æthiopian with his army issued forth against them, ten hundred thousand, and with three hundred chariots: and he came as far as Maresa.

text.NRSV: Asa went out to meet him, and they drew up their lines of battle in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.
text.Rotherham: And Asa went out to meet him,—and they set in array for battle, in the valley of Zaphonah, at Mareshah.
text.KJV: 14:10 Then Asa went out against him, and they set the battel in aray in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.
text.DRV: 14:10 Moreover Asa went on to meet him, and set his army in array to battle in the vale Sephata, which is near Maresa.

text.NRSV: Asa cried to the Lord his God, “O Lord, there is no difference through you between helping the mighty and the weak. Help us, O Lord our God, for we rely on you, and in your name we have come against this multitude. O Lord, you are our God; let no mortal prevail against you.”
text.Rotherham: Then Asa cried out unto Yahweh his God, and said, O Yahweh, it is, nothing with thee, to help whether with many or with such as have no strength. Help us, O Yahweh our God, for, on thee, do we lean, and, in thy name, have we come against this multitude,—O Yahweh! our God, thou art, let not, weak man, have power against thee.
text.KJV: 14:11 And Asa cried vnto the Lord his God, and said, Lord, it is nothing with thee to helpe, whether with many, or with them that haue no power. Helpe vs, O Lord our God, for we rest on thee, and in thy Name wee goe against this multitude: O Lord thou art our God, let not man preuaile against thee.
text.DRV: 14:11 And he invocated our Lord God, and said: Lord there is no difference with thee, whether thou help in few, or in many: help us o Lord our God: for having confidence in thee, & in thy name we are come against this multitude. Lord, thou art our God, let not man prevail against thee.

text.NRSV: So the Lord defeated the Ethiopians before Asa and before Judah, and the Ethiopians fled.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh smote the Ethiopians, before Asa, and before Judah,—and the Ethiopians fled.
text.KJV: 14:12 So the Lord smote the Ethiopians before Asa, and before Iudah, and the Ethiopians fled.
text.DRV: 14:12 Our Lord therefore terrified the Æthiopians before Asa and Juda: & the Æthiopians fled.

text.NRSV: Asa and the army with him pursued them as far as Gerar, and the Ethiopians fell until not one remained alive; for they were broken before the Lord and his army. The people of Judah carried away a great quantity of booty.
text.Rotherham: And Asa and the people that were with him pursued as far as to Gerar, and the Ethiopians fell, so that there was no way for them to recover, for they were routed before Yahweh, and before him host,—and they carried away exceeding much spoil.
text.KJV: 14:13 And Asa and the people that were with him, pursued them vnto Gerar: and the Ethiopians were ouerthrown, that they could not recouer themselues, for they were destroyed before the Lord, and before his hoste, and they caried away very much spoile.
text.DRV: 14:13 And Asa pursued them, & the people that was with him, unto Gerara: & the Æthiopians fell to utter destruction because our Lord killing them, and his army fighting, they were destroyed. They took therefore many spoils,

text.NRSV: They struck all the cities around Gerar, for the fear of the Lord was on them. They plundered all the cities, for there was much plunder in them.
text.Rotherham: And they smote all the cities round shout Gerar, for the dread of Yahweh was upon them,—and they plundered all the cities, for, great plunder, was there in them.
text.KJV: 14:14 And they smote all the cities round about Gerar, for the feare of the Lord came vpon them: and they spoiled all the cities, for there was exceeding much spoile in them.
text.DRV: 14:14 and they struck all the cities round about Gerara: for great terror had invaded all men: and they spoiled the cities, and carried away much prey.

text.NRSV: They also struck the tents of those who had livestock, and carried away sheep and camels in abundance. Then they returned to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, the tents of the cattle, they smote,—and carried off sheep in abundance, and camels, and returned to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 14:15 They smote also the tents of cattell, and caried away sheepe and camels inabundance, and returned to Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 14:15 Yea and destroying the sheepcotes of sheep, they took an infinite multitude of cattle, and of camels: and returned into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: The spirit of God came upon Azariah son of Oded.
text.Rotherham: Now, as for Azariah son of Oded, there came upon him, the spirit of God.
text.KJV: 15:1 And the Spirit of God came vpon Azariah the sonne of Oded.
text.DRV: 15:1 And Azarias the son of Oded, the Spirit of God coming upon him,

text.NRSV: He went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: The Lord is with you, while you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you abandon him, he will abandon you.
text.Rotherham: So he went forth to meet Asa, and said unto him, Hear me, O Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin,—Yahweh, is with you, so long as ye are with him, and, if ye seek him, he will be found of you, but, if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.
text.KJV: 15:2 And he went out to meet Asa, and said vnto him, Heare ye me, Asa, and all Iudah, and Beniamin, The Lord is with you, while yee be with him: and if yee seeke him, he will be found of you: but if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.
text.DRV: 15:2 went out to meet Asa, and said to him: Hear ye me Asa, and all Juda and Benjamin: Our Lord is with you, because you have been with him. If you will seek him, you shall find: but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.

text.NRSV: For a long time Israel was without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law;
text.Rotherham: Now, many days, had Israel been,—without the faithful God, and without a teaching priest, and without the law;
text.KJV: 15:3 Now for a long season Israel hath bene without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law.
text.DRV: 15:3 And many days shall pass in Israel without the true God, and without Priest a teacher, and without the Law.

text.NRSV: but when in their distress they turned to the Lord, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.
text.Rotherham: But they turned, in their distress, unto Yahweh God of Israel,—and sought him, and he was found of them.
text.KJV: 15:4 But when they in their trouble did turne vnto the Lord God of Israel, and sought him, hee was found of them.
text.DRV: 15:4 And when they shall return in their distress to our Lord the God of Israel, and shall seek him, they shall find him.

text.NRSV: In those times there was no peace for those who went out or for those who came in, for great disturbance afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
text.Rotherham: And, in those times, there had been no prosperity, to him that went out nor to him that came in,—for, great consternations, were upon all the inhabitants of the lands;
text.KJV: 15:5 And in those times there was no peace to him that went out, nor to him that came in, but great vexations were vpon all the inhabitants of the countreys.
text.DRV: 15:5 At that time there shall not be peace to him that goeth out and cometh in, but terrors on every side in the inhabiters of the earth.

text.NRSV: They were broken in pieces, nation against nation and city against city, for God troubled them with every sort of distress.
text.Rotherham: and they were beaten in pieces-nation against nation, and city against city,—for, God, discomfited them with all manner of distress.
text.KJV: 15:6 And nation was destroyed of nation, and citie of citie: for God did vexe them with all aduersitie.
text.DRV: 15:6 For nation shall fight against nation, and city against city, because our Lord will trouble them with all distress.

text.NRSV: But you, take courage! Do not let your hands be weak, for your work shall be rewarded.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be strong, and let not your hands be slack,—for there is a reward for your work!
text.KJV: 15:7 Be ye strong therefore, and let not your hands bee weake: for your worke shall be rewarded.
text.DRV: 15:7 You therefore take courage, and let not your hands be dissolved: for there shall be reward to your work.

text.NRSV: When Asa heard these words, the prophecy of Azariah son of Oded, he took courage, and put away the abominable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities that he had taken in the hill country of Ephraim. He repaired the altar of the Lord that was in front of the vestibule of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, when Asa heard these words and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he strengthened himself, and put away the abominations out of all the land of Judah and Benjamin, and out of the cities which he had captured out of the hill country of Ephraim,—and renewed the altar of Yahweh, that was before the porch of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:8 And when Asa heard these words, and the prophesie of Oded the prophet, he tooke courage, and put away the abominable idoles out of all the lande of Iudah and Beniamin, and out of the cities which hee had taken from mount Ephraim, and renewed the Altar of the Lord, that was before the porch of the Lord.
text.DRV: 15:8 Which when Asa had heard, to wit, the words, and the prophecy of Azarias the son of Oded the Prophet, he took courage, and took away the idols out of all the land of Juda, and out of Benjamin, and out of the cities, which he had taken, of mount Ephraim, and he dedicated the altar of our Lord, which was before the porch of our Lord.

text.NRSV: He gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and those from Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon who were residing as aliens with them; for great numbers had deserted to him from Israel when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.
text.Rotherham: And he gathered together all Judah and Benjamin, and the sojourners with them, out of Ephraim and out of Manasseh, and out of Simeon,—for they fell unto him out of Israel, in great numbers, because they saw that, Yahweh his God, was with him.
text.KJV: 15:9 And he gathered all Iudah and Beniamin, and the strangers with them out of Ephraim and Manasseh, and out of Simeon: (for they fell to him out of Israel in abundance when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.)
text.DRV: 15:9 And he gathered together all Juda and Benjamin, & the strangers with them of Ephraim, and of Manasses, and of Simeon: for many were fled to him of Israel, seeing that our Lord his God was with him.

text.NRSV: They were gathered at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
text.Rotherham: So they gathered themselves together unto Jerusalem, in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
text.KJV: 15:10 So they gathered themselues together at Ierusalem, in the third moneth, in the fifteenth yeere of the reigne of Asa.
text.DRV: 15:10 And when they were come into Jerusalem the third month, in the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa,

text.NRSV: They sacrificed to the Lord on that day, from the booty that they had brought, seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep.
text.Rotherham: And they sacrificed unto Yahweh, on that day, out of the spoil they had brought in,—oxen, seven hundred, and sheep, seven thousand.
text.KJV: 15:11 And they offered vnto the Lord the same time, of the spoile which they had brought, seuen hundred oxen, and seuen thousand sheepe.
text.DRV: 15:11 they immolated to our Lord in that day of the spoils and the prey that they had brought, oxen seven hundred, and rams seven thousand.

text.NRSV: They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and with all their soul.
text.Rotherham: And they entered into a covenant, to seek Yahweh, God of their fathers,—with all their heart, and with all their soul;
text.KJV: 15:12 And they entred into a couenant to seeke the Lord God of their fathers, with all their heart and with all their soule:
text.DRV: 15:12 And he went in after the manner to establish the covenant, that they should seek our Lord the God of their fathers in all their heart, and in all their soul.

text.NRSV: Whoever would not seek the Lord, the God of Israel, should be put to death, whether young or old, man or woman.
text.Rotherham: and, whosoever would not seek unto Yahweh God of Israel, should be put to death,—whether small or great, whether man or woman.
text.KJV: 15:13 That whosoeuer would not seeke the Lord God of Israel, should be put to death, whether small or great, whether man or woman.
text.DRV: 15:13 And if any man, quoth he, shall not seek our Lord the God of Israel, let him die, from the least to the greatest, from man unto woman.

text.NRSV: They uttered an oath to the Lord with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with horns.
text.Rotherham: So they bound themselves by oath unto Yahweh, with a loud voice, and with shouting,—and with trumpets and with horns.
text.KJV: 15:14 And they sware vnto the Lord with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with cornets.
text.DRV: 15:14 And they sware to our Lord with a loud voice in jubilation, and in noise of trumpet, and sound of shalms,

text.NRSV: All Judah rejoiced over the oath; for they had sworn with all their heart, and had sought him with their whole desire, and he was found by them, and the Lord gave them rest on every side.
text.Rotherham: And all Judah rejoiced over the oath, for, with all their heart, had they sworn, and, with all their good will, had they sought him, and he was found of them,—and Yahweh gave them rest round about.
text.KJV: 15:15 And all Iudah reioyced at the oath: for they had sworne with all their heart, & sought him with their whole desire, and he was found of them: and the Lord gaue them rest round about.
text.DRV: 15:15 all that were in Juda, with execration: for in all their heart did they swear, & with all their will did they seek him, & found him, & our Lord gave them rest round about.

text.NRSV: King Asa even deprived his mother Maacah of her dignity as queen mother, because she had made an abominable image for Asherah. Asa cut down her image, crushed it, and burned it at the Wadi Kidron.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, as touching Maacah mother of Asa the king, he removed her from being queen, because she had made, unto the Sacred Stem, a monstrous thing,—so Asa cut down her monstrous thing, and reduced it to dust, and burnt it up, in the Kidron ravine.
text.KJV: 15:16 ¶ And also concerning Maachah the mother of Asa the king, he remooued her from beeing Queene, because she had made an idole in a groue: and Asa cut downe her idole, and stamped it, and burnt it at the brooke Kidron.
text.DRV: 15:16 Yea & Maacha the mother of king Asa he deposed from the royal empire, because she had made in a grove the idol of Priapus: which he wholly destroyed, and breaking into pieces, burnt it in the Torrent cedron.

text.NRSV: But the high places were not removed from Israel. Nevertheless the heart of Asa was true all his days.
text.Rotherham: But, the high places, were not taken away out of Israel,—only, the heart of Asa, was perfect, all his days.
text.KJV: 15:17 But the high places were not taken away out of Israel: neuerthelesse the heart of Asa was perfect all his dayes.
text.DRV: 15:17 But the Excelses were left in Israel: nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.

text.NRSV: He brought into the house of God the votive gifts of his father and his own votive gifts—silver, gold, and utensils.
text.Rotherham: And he brought the hallowed things of his father and his own hallowed things, into the house of God,—silver and gold, and utensils.
text.KJV: 15:18 ¶ And he brought into the house of God the things that his father had dedicated, and that he himselfe had dedicated, siluer, and gold, and vessels.
text.DRV: 15:18 And those things which his father had vowed, and himself, he brought into the house of our Lord, gold and silver, and of vessels diverse furniture.

text.NRSV: And there was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
text.Rotherham: And, war, was there none,—until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
text.KJV: 15:19 And there was no more warre vnto the fiue and thirtieth yeere of the reigne of Asa.
text.DRV: 15:19 And there was no war unto the five and thirtieth year of the reign of Asa.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-sixth year of the reign of Asa, King Baasha of Israel went up against Judah, and built Ramah, to prevent anyone from going out or coming into the territory of King Asa of Judah.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-sixth year of the reign of Asa, Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah, and built Ramah,—that he might let none come out or go in, unto Asa king of Judah.
text.KJV: 16:1 In the sixe and thirtieth yeere of the reigne of Asa, Baasha king of Israel came vp against Iudah, and built Ramah, to the intent that hee might let none goe out or come in to Asa king of Iudah.
text.DRV: 16:1 And in the six and thirtieth year of his reign, came up Baasa the king of Israel into Juda, and with a wall compassed Rama, that none could safely go out and come in of the Kingdom of Asa.

text.NRSV: Then Asa took silver and gold from the treasures of the house of the Lord and the king’s house, and sent them to King Ben-hadad of Aram, who resided in Damascus, saying,
text.Rotherham: So Asa brought forth silver and gold, out of the treasuries of the house of Yahweh, and the house of the king,—and sent unto Ben-hadad, king of Syria, who dwelt in Damascus, saying:
text.KJV: 16:2 Then Asa brought out siluer and golde out of the treasures of the house of the Lord, and of the kings house, and sent to Benhadad King of Syria that dwelt at Damascus, saying;
text.DRV: 16:2 Asa therefore brought forth silver and gold, out of the treasures house of our Lord, and of the king's treasures, and he sent to Benadad the king of Syria, who dwelt in Damascus, saying:

text.NRSV: “Let there be an alliance between me and you, like that between my father and your father; I am sending to you silver and gold; go, break your alliance with King Baasha of Israel, so that he may withdraw from me.”
text.Rotherham: [Let there be] a covenant, between me and thee, as between my father and thy father.—Lo! I have sent thee silver and gold, go break thy covenant with Baasha king of Israel, that he may go up from me.
text.KJV: 16:3 There is a league betweene me and thee, as there was betweene my father and thy father: beholde, I haue sent thee siluer and golde, goe, breake thy league with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.
text.DRV: 16:3 There is league between me and thee, my father also & thy father had concord, wherefore I have sent thee silver and gold that breaking the league, which thou hast with Baasa the king of Israel, thou make him retire from me.

text.NRSV: Ben-hadad listened to King Asa, and sent the commanders of his armies against the cities of Israel. They conquered Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, and all the storage cities of Naphtali.
text.Rotherham: And Ben-hadad hearkened unto King Asa, and sent the chieftains of the forces which he had, against the cities of Israel, and smote Ijon and Dan, and Abel-maim,—and all the storehouses of the cities of Naphtali.
text.KJV: 16:4 And Benhadad hearkened vnto king Asa, and sent the captaines of his armies against the cities of Israel; and they smote Iion, and Dan, and Abelmaim, & all the store-cities of Naphtali.
text.DRV: 16:4 Which being known, Benadad sent the Princes of his hosts to the cities of Israel: who struck Ahion, and Dan, and Ablemaim, and all the walled cities of Nephthali.

text.NRSV: When Baasha heard of it, he stopped building Ramah, and let his work cease.
text.Rotherham: And it cams to pass, when Baasha heard it, he left off building Ramah,—and let his work cease.
text.KJV: 16:5 And it came to passe, when Baasha heard it, that hee left off building of Ramah, and let his worke cease.
text.DRV: 16:5 Which when Baasa had heard, he ceased to build Rama, & intermitted his work.

text.NRSV: Then King Asa brought all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Ramah and its timber, with which Baasha had been building, and with them he built Geba and Mizpah.
text.Rotherham: And, Asa the king, took all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Ramah, and the timbers thereof, which Baasha had used in building,—and he built therewith, Geba and Mizpah.
text.KJV: 16:6 Then Asa the king tooke all Iudah, and they caried away the stones of Ramah, and the timber thereof, wherewith Baasha was a building, and hee built there with Geba and Mizpah.
text.DRV: 16:6 Moreover Asa the king took all Juda, and carried away the stones out of Rama, and the timber that Baasa had prepared for the building: & he built of them Gabaa, & Maspha.

text.NRSV: At that time the seer Hanani came to King Asa of Judah, and said to him, “Because you relied on the king of Aram, and did not rely on the Lord your God, the army of the king of Aram has escaped you.
text.Rotherham: And, at that time, came Hanani the seer, unto Asa the king of Judah,—and said unto him, Because thou hast leaned upon the king of Syria, and hast not leaned upon Yahweh thy God, for this cause, hath the force of the king of Syria escaped out of thy hand.
text.KJV: 16:7 ¶ And at that time Hanani the Seer came to Asa king of Iudah, and said vnto him, Because thou hast relyed on the king of Syria, and not relyed on the Lord thy God, therefore is the hoste of the king of Syria escaped out of thine hand.
text.DRV: 16:7 At that time came Hanani the Prophet to Asa the king of Juda, & said to him: Because thou hast had confidence in the king of Syria, and not in our Lord thy God, therefore hath the army of the king of Syria escaped out of thy hand.

text.NRSV: Were not the Ethiopians and the Libyans a huge army with exceedingly many chariots and cavalry? Yet because you relied on the Lord, he delivered them into your hand.
text.Rotherham: Were not, the Ethiopians and the Lybians, a huge host, with chariots and horsemen exceeding many? yet, because thou didst lean upon Yahweh, he delivered them into thy hand.
text.KJV: 16:8 Were not the Ethiopians and the Lubims a huge hoste, with very many charets and horsemen? Yet because thou diddest relie on the Lord, he deliuered them into thine hand.
text.DRV: 16:8 Were not the Æthiopians, and Libyans, many more in chariots and horsemen, and a multitude exceeding great: whom, when thou didst believe in our Lord, he delivered into thy hand?

text.NRSV: For the eyes of the Lord range throughout the entire earth, to strengthen those whose heart is true to him. You have done foolishly in this; for from now on you will have wars.”
text.Rotherham: For, as touching Yahweh, [seeing that], his eyes, are ever running to and fro throughout all the earth, to shew himself strong with them who are perfect toward himself, thou hast made thyself foolish over this,—for, from henceforth, there shall be with thee—wars.
text.KJV: 16:9 For the eyes of the Lord run to and fro throughout the whole earth, to shewe himselfe strong in the behalfe of them, whose heart is perfite towards him. Herein thou hast done foolishly; therefore, from hencefoorth thou shalt haue warres.
text.DRV: 16:9 For the eyes of our Lord behold all the earth, and give strength to them, that with perfect heart believe in him. Thou therefore hast done foolishly, and for this cause from this present time shall battles arise against thee.

text.NRSV: Then Asa was angry with the seer, and put him in the stocks, in prison, for he was in a rage with him because of this. And Asa inflicted cruelties on some of the people at the same time.
text.Rotherham: Then was Asa angry with the seer, and put him in the house of the stocks, for he was in a rage with him, over this,—and Asa oppressed some of the people, at that time.
text.KJV: 16:10 Then Asa was wroth with the Seer, and put him in a prison-house; for he was in a rage with him because of this thing. And Asa oppressed some of the people the same time.
text.DRV: 16:10 And Asa being angry against the Seer, commanded him to be cast into fetters: for he took indignation exceedingly upon this thing: & he slew of the people at that time very many.

text.NRSV: The acts of Asa, from first to last, are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.
text.Rotherham: But lo! the story of Asa, first and last, there it is, written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
text.KJV: 16:11 ¶ And behold, the actes of Asa first and last, lo, they are written in the booke of the Kings of Iudah and Israel.
text.DRV: 16:11 But the works of Asa the first & the last are written in the Book of the kings of Juda and Israel.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-ninth year of his reign Asa was diseased in his feet, and his disease became severe; yet even in his disease he did not seek the Lord, but sought help from physicians.
text.Rotherham: And Asa became diseased—in the thirty-ninth year of his reign—in his feet, exceedingly severe, was his disease,—yet, even in his disease, he sought not Yahweh, but unto physicians.
text.KJV: 16:12 And Asa in the thirtie and ninth yeere of his reigne, was diseased in his feete, vntill his disease was exceeding great: yet in his disease hee sought not to the Lord, but to the Physicians.
text.DRV: 16:12 Asa also fell sick in the nine and thirtieth year of his reign, of a most vehement pain of his feet, and neither in his infirmity did he seek our Lord, but rather trusted in the art of Physicians.

text.NRSV: Asa slept with his ancestors, dying in the forty-first year of his reign.
text.Rotherham: And Asa slept with his fathers,—yea he died in the forty-first year of his reign;
text.KJV: 16:13 ¶ And Asa slept with his fathers, and died in the one and fourtieth yeere of his reigne.
text.DRV: 16:13 And he slept with his fathers: and he died the one and fortieth year of his reign.

text.NRSV: They buried him in the tomb that he had hewn out for himself in the city of David. They laid him on a bier that had been filled with various kinds of spices prepared by the perfumer’s art; and they made a very great fire in his honor.
text.Rotherham: and they buried him in his own stately sepulchre, which he had hewn for himself in the city of David, and laid him on a couch which was full of sweet spices—yea of various kinds, made by the perfumer’s art,—and they burned for him with an exceeding great burning.
text.KJV: 16:14 And they buried him in his owne sepulchres which he had made for himselfe in the citie of Dauid, and laide him in the bed, which was filled with sweet odours, and diuers kindes of spices prepared by the Apothecaries arte: & they made a very great burning for him.
text.DRV: 16:14 And they buried him in his sepulchre, which he had digged for himself in the City of David: and they laid him upon his bed full of spices and odoriferous ointments, which were made by the art of apothecaries, and they burnt over him with exceeding ambition.

text.NRSV: His son Jehoshaphat succeeded him, and strengthened himself against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead,—and strengthened himself against Israel;
text.KJV: 17:1 And Iehoshaphat his sonne reigned in his stead, and strengthened himselfe against Israel.
text.DRV: 17:1 And Josaphat his son reigned for him, and grew strong against Israel.

text.NRSV: He placed forces in all the fortified cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim that his father Asa had taken.
text.Rotherham: and put forces in all the fortified cities of Judah,—and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had captured.
text.KJV: 17:2 And he placed forces in all the fenced cities of Iudah, and set garisons in the land of Iudah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.
text.DRV: 17:2 And he appointed numbers of soldiers in all the cities of Juda, that were compassed with walls. And he placed garrisons in the land of Juda, & in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.

text.NRSV: The Lord was with Jehoshaphat, because he walked in the earlier ways of his father; he did not seek the Baals,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, Yahweh, was with Jehoshaphat,—because he walked in the first ways of David his father, and sought not unto the Baalim;
text.KJV: 17:3 And the Lord was with Iehoshaphat, because hee walked in the first wayes of his father Dauid, and sought not vnto Baalim:
text.DRV: 17:3 And our Lord was with Josaphat, because he walked in the first ways of David his father: and he trusted not in Baalim,

text.NRSV: but sought the God of his father and walked in his commandments, and not according to the ways of Israel.
text.Rotherham: for, unto the God of his father, he sought, and, in his commandments, he walked,—and not according to the doing of Israel.
text.KJV: 17:4 But sought to the Lord God of his father, and walked in his commandements, and not after the doings of Israel:
text.DRV: 17:4 but in the God of his father, & went forward in his precepts, and not according to the sins of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord established the kingdom in his hand. All Judah brought tribute to Jehoshaphat, and he had great riches and honor.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did Yahweh establish the kingdom in his hand, and all Judah gave a present unto Jehoshaphat,—and he came to have riches and honour, in abundance.
text.KJV: 17:5 Therefore the Lord stablished the kingdome in his hand, and all Iudah brought to Iehoshaphat presents, and he had riches and honour in abundance.
text.DRV: 17:5 And our Lord confirmed the Kingdom in his hand, and all Juda gave gifts to Josaphat: and there grew to him infinite riches, and much glory.

text.NRSV: His heart was courageous in the ways of the Lord; and furthermore he removed the high places and the sacred poles from Judah.
text.Rotherham: And his heart was encouraged, in the ways of Yahweh,—and he, yet further, took away the high places and the Sacred Stems, out of Judah.
text.KJV: 17:6 And his heart was lift vp in the wayes of the Lord: moreouer hee tooke away the high places and groues out of Iudah.
text.DRV: 17:6 And when his heart had taken courage for the ways of our Lord, he took away also the Excelses and groves out of Juda.

text.NRSV: In the third year of his reign he sent his officials, Ben-hail, Obadiah, Zechariah, Nethanel, and Micaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And, in the third year of his reign, he sent to his rulers, even to Ben-hail and to Obadiah and to Zechariah, and to Nethanel, and to Micaiah,—that they were to teach throughout the cities of Judah;
text.KJV: 17:7 ¶ Also in the third yeere of his reigne, hee sent to his princes, euen to Benhail, and to Obadiah, and to Zechariah, and to Nethaneel, and to Michaiah, to teach in the cities of Iudah:
text.DRV: 17:7 And in the third year of his Kingdom, he sent of his Princes Benhail, and Abdias, and Zacharias, and Nathanael, and Micheas, that they should teach in the cities of Juda:

text.NRSV: With them were the Levites, Shemaiah, Nethaniah, Zebadiah, Asahel, Shemiramoth, Jehonathan, Adonijah, Tobijah, and Tob-adonijah; and with these Levites, the priests Elishama and Jehoram.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, Levites, Shemaiah and Nethaniah and Zebadiah and Asahel and Shemiramoth and Jehonathan, and Adonijah and Tobijah and Tob-adonijah, Levites,—and, with them, Elishama and Jehoram, priests;
text.KJV: 17:8 And with them hee sent Leuites, euen Shemaiah, and Nethaniah, and Zebadiah, and Asahel, and Shemiramoth, and Iehonathan, and Adoniiah, and Tobiiah, and Tob-adoniiah, Leuites: and with them, Elishama and Iehoram, Priests.
text.DRV: 17:8 and with them Levites, Semeias, & Nathanias, and Zabadias, Asael also, and Semiramoth, and Jonathan, and Adonias, and Tobias, and Thobadonias Levites, and with them Elisama, and Joram Priests.

text.NRSV: They taught in Judah, having the book of the law of the Lord with them; they went around through all the cities of Judah and taught among the people.
text.Rotherham: and they taught throughout Judah, and, with them, was the book of the law of Yahweh,—so they went round throughout all the cities of Judah, and taught the people.
text.KJV: 17:9 And they taught in Iudah, and had the book of the Law of the Lord with them, and went about throughout all the cities of Iudah, and taught the people.
text.DRV: 17:9 And they taught the people in Juda, having the book of the law of our Lord: and they went about all the cities of Juda, and instructed the people.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord fell on all the kingdoms of the lands around Judah, and they did not make war against Jehoshaphat.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, the dread of Yahweh, was upon all the kingdoms of the countries, that were round about Judah,—and they warred not against Jehoshaphat.
text.KJV: 17:10 ¶ And the feare of the Lord fell vpon all the kingdomes of the lands that were round about Iudah, so that they made no warre against Iehoshaphat.
text.DRV: 17:10 Therefore the dread of our Lord came upon all the Kingdoms of the lands, that were round about Juda, neither durst they make battle against Josaphat.

text.NRSV: Some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents, and silver for tribute; and the Arabs also brought him livestock, seven thousand seven hundred rams and seven thousand seven hundred male goats.
text.Rotherham: And, from among the Philistines, were they bringing in unto Jehoshaphat a present, and silver as tribute,—even the Arabians, were bringing in unto him flocks, seven thousand seven hundred rams, and seven thousand seven hundred he-goats.
text.KJV: 17:11 Also some of the Philistines brought Iehoshaphat presents, and tribute siluer, and the Arabians brought him flocks, seuen thousand and seuen hundred rammes, and seuen thousand and seuen hundred he goats.
text.DRV: 17:11 Yea and the Philistians brought gifts to Josaphat, and tribute of silver, the Arabians also brought cattle, of rams seven thousand seven hundred, and buck goats as many.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat grew steadily greater. He built fortresses and storage cities in Judah.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass that, Jehoshaphat, went on waxing surpassingly great,—and he built, throughout Judah, fortresses and cities for store;
text.KJV: 17:12 ¶ And Iehoshaphat waxed great exceedingly, and he built in Iudah castles, and cities of store.
text.DRV: 17:12 Josaphat therefore grew, & was magnified on high: and he built in Juda houses like to towers, and walled cities.

text.NRSV: He carried out great works in the cities of Judah. He had mighty warriors in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, much business, had he, throughout the cities of Judah,—but, the men of war, the heroes of valour, were in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 17:13 And he had much businesse in the cities of Iudah: and the men of warre, mightie men of valour, were in Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 17:13 And he prepared many works in the cities of Juda: there were also men of war, and valiant in Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: This was the muster of them by ancestral houses: Of Judah, the commanders of thousands: Adnah the commander, with three hundred thousand mighty warriors;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the numbers of them, by their ancestral house,—To Judah, pertained rulers of thousands, Adnah the chief, and, with him, mighty men of valour, three hundred thousand;
text.KJV: 17:14 And these are the numbers of them according to the house of their fathers: Of Iudah, the captaines of thousands, Adnah the chiefe, and with him mighty men of valour, three hundred thousand.
text.DRV: 17:14 of whom this is the number by the houses and families of every one: In Juda Princes of the army, Ednas duke, and with him most valiant men three hundred thousand.

text.NRSV: and next to him Jehohanan the commander, with two hundred eighty thousand;
text.Rotherham: and, under his direction, was Jehohanan the chief,—and, with him, two hundred and eighty thousand;
text.KJV: 17:15 And next to him was Iehohanan the captaine, and with him two hundred and fourescore thousand.
text.DRV: 17:15 After him Johanan the Prince, and with him two hundred eighty thousand.

text.NRSV: and next to him Amasiah son of Zichri, a volunteer for the service of the Lord, with two hundred thousand mighty warriors.
text.Rotherham: and, under his direction, Amasiah son of Zichri, who willingly offered himself unto Yahweh,—and, with him, two hundred thousand heroes of valour.
text.KJV: 17:16 And next him was Amasiah the sonne of Zichri, who willingly offered himselfe vnto the Lord, and with him two hundred thousand mightie men of valour.
text.DRV: 17:16 After him also Amasias the son of Zechri, consecrated to our Lord, and with him two hundred thousand of valiant men.

text.NRSV: Of Benjamin: Eliada, a mighty warrior, with two hundred thousand armed with bow and shield;
